2603.22709.title: Who Spoke What When? Evaluating Spoken Language Models for Conversational ASR with Semantic and Overlap-Aware Metrics
2603.22709.paper_summary: Conversational automatic speech recognition remains challenging due to overlapping speech, far-field noise, and varying speaker counts. While recent LLM-based systems perform well on single-speaker benchmarks, their robustness in multi-speaker settings is unclear. We systematically compare LLM-based and modular pipeline approaches along four axes: overlap robustness, semantic fidelity, speaker count, and single- versus multi-channel input. To capture meaning-altering errors that conventional metrics miss, we introduce tcpSemER, which extends tcpWER by replacing Levenshtein distance with embedding-based semantic similarity. We further decompose tcpWER into overlapping and non-overlapping components for finer-grained analysis. Experiments across three datasets show that LLM-based systems are competitive in two-speaker settings but degrade as speaker count and overlap increase, whereas modular pipelines remain more robust.
2603.22709.paper_summary_zh: 對話式自動語音識別仍然面臨挑戰，原因包括重疊語音、遠場噪音和變化的說話者數量。雖然最近基於LLM的系統在單一說話者基準上表現良好，但它們在多說話者環境中的穩健性尚不明確。我們沿著四個軸線系統地比較基於LLM的和模組化管道的方法：重疊穩健性、語義忠實度、說話者數量，以及單通道與多通道輸入。為了捕捉傳統指標所忽略的意義改變錯誤，我們引入tcpSemER，通過用基於嵌入的語義相似性替換Levenshtein距離來擴展tcpWER。我們進一步將tcpWER分解為重疊和非重疊組件，以進行更細緻的分析。三個數據集的實驗顯示，基於LLM的系統在雙說話者環境中具有競爭力，但隨著說話者數量和重疊的增加而退化，而模組化管道則保持更高的穩健性。
2603.22709.author: Naohiro Tawara et.al.
2603.22709.authors: Naohiro Tawara, Samuele Cornell, Alexander Polok, Marc Delcroix, Lukáš Burget, Shinji Watanabe
